# LinkedIn Draft - 2026-07-02

Claude Sonnet 5 offers performance close to Opus 4.8 at a lower price point, with intentionally limited cyber capability versus Mythos 5.

Went through 40 links today. Here's what made the brief:

1. What's new in Claude Sonnet 5
   Direct read on whether to move workloads to Sonnet 5 for cost savings, from a trusted, hands-on source.

2. Vercel Agent expands to production investigations and approved actions (public beta)
   Concrete reference design for agentic incident response with a real approval/permission model.

3. Vercel Functions now run arbitrary Dockerfiles on Fluid Compute
   Meaningfully widens what can run on Vercel's serverless platform - relevant if you were avoiding Vercel for non-JS/Python workloads.

4. Claude Code caught steganographically marking requests based on hidden domain/timezone list
   Credible, reproducible trust/transparency issue in a tool many builders (including this pipeline's operator) use daily.

5. Nano Banana 2 Lite image model lands on Vercel AI Gateway at $0.034/image
   Concrete cost figure for cheap, fast image generation - useful if budgeting an image-gen feature.

Full brief with all 40 researched links → https://thenightlylibrarian.com/briefs/2026-07-02
